perturbation.original_text: THI shall prepay Marv for any affirmed IP Cost before payment is to be made by Marv. Marv shall have no duty to pay an IP Cost, whether affirmed or not affirmed, for which Marv does not receive prepayment. If THI does not pay Marv by the Due Date, the Application or Patent shall revert to Marv as if THI had denied payment under section 2(c)3.
perturbation.changed_text: THI shall prepay Marv for any affirmed IP Cost before payment is to be made by Marv. Marv shall have the discretion to pay an IP Cost, whether affirmed or not affirmed, regardless of prepayment, based on the strategic importance of the Intellectual Property. If THI does not pay Marv by the Due Date, the Application or Patent shall revert to Marv as if THI had denied payment under section 2(c)3.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding Marv's payment obligations. Originally, Marv had no duty to pay without prepayment. Now, Marv has the discretion to pay regardless of prepayment, creating a contradiction. This directly contradicts the later part of Section 2(c)4, which states the patent reverts to Marv if THI doesn't pay, implying Marv had no independent payment obligation. This introduces an in-text contradiction on whether Marv is responsible to pay or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: THI shall prepay Marv for any affirmed IP Cost before payment is to be made by Marv. Marv shall have the discretion to pay an IP Cost, whether affirmed or not affirmed, regardless of prepayment, based on the strategic importance of the Intellectual Property. If THI does not pay Marv by the Due Date, the Application or Patent shall revert to Marv as if THI had denied payment under section 2(c)3.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants Marv the discretion to pay IP costs regardless of prepayment, which directly contradicts the original text's implication that Marv has 'no duty to pay' without prepayment from THI. This introduces ambiguity and a strong in-text contradiction. Furthermore, the altered section contradicts itself, as after stating Marv has discretion to pay regardless of prepayment it then states 'If THI does not pay Marv by the Due Date, the Application or Patent shall revert to Marv as if THI had denied payment under section 2(c)3', implying Marv does not have an independent obligation to pay.

perturbation.original_text: Royalty payments are payable from THI to Marv Enterprises, LLC and will be in the amount of 5% of the Fair Market Value of: a. Licensed Product that is sold, leased or put into use by the THI or any Related Companies in the preceding calendar quarter; and b. any service performed by THI or any Related Companies that directly or indirectly uses Licensed Product.
perturbation.changed_text: Royalty payments are payable from THI to Marv Enterprises, LLC and will be in the amount of up to 5% of the Fair Market Value of: a. Licensed Product that is sold, leased or put into use by the THI or any Related Companies in the preceding calendar quarter; and b. any service performed by THI or any Related Companies that directly or indirectly uses Licensed Product. The specific percentage will be determined annually by Marv, based on their assessment of market conditions.
perturbation.explanation: By changing '5%' to 'up to 5%', and introducing that the percentage will be determined by Marv based on market conditions, introduces an ambiguity. The definition originally suggested a fixed rate, which is now variable at Marv's discretion. Later in the section, calculating the addition 5%, the language clearly states it is an addition to the 5% already agreed between the parties, this modification contradicts this section. THI is now uncertain of how much royalty payment need to be made, since now the percentage may vary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition to the 5% amount calculated pursuant to the preceding paragraph, there shall be an identical 5% amount to be paid from THI, per Premier's consideration, to certain shareholders of Premier, as identified by Premier, as of a record date to be determined in the future, on an annual basis, commencing on the one-year anniversary of this Agreement until a total amount of $40,000,000 has been paid to Premier. This royalty is separate and distinct from the royalty obligation to Marv as stated in 2(d)1.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a significant ambiguity by changing '5%' to 'up to 5%' and granting Marv the discretion to determine the percentage based on market conditions. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which refers to 'In addition to the 5% amount calculated pursuant to the preceding paragraph'. The original text made the 5% a fixed amount. The changed text introduces uncertainty about the royalty payment and causes an in-text contradiction.
